lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there. What are you lot doing out here?

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: We're from the College of Bards in Tempestus. We thought we'd set up camp here to do some performances for anyone travelling through the savannah.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: With the kingdom now open to tourists, we've had plenty of new fans!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice trumpet.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Thanks! I made it myself. The College of Bards insist that we all have the ability to create and maintain our own instruments. I'm quite proud of what I came up with!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I hope it serves you well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Think you've got what it takes to join the College of Bards?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Well it only takes a lifetime of practice, so maybe give it a go one day.

sections.title: The one that is not singing

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there. Are you enjoying the view?

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: I am, though I do wish it would be blocked by the ferry to Tempestus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The ferry? Is there something wrong with it?

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: I'm not sure exactly. All I know is that it's not running at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How urgently do you need to get there?

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: We should be back at the College of Bards already, but I'm sure they'll understand why we got delayed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, you're a bard? Could you sing for me?

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Maybe. Can you take us to Temptestus?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Unfortunately not.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Then I'm afraid you don't get any singing this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Nilsal! Have you come to hear a song from the Sister Songstresses of the College of Bards?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sister Songstresses? So you're a group of sisters who sing together?

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Don't be silly! Of course we aren't!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... The name did suggest otherwise...

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Well, we realised when we met that we were all very similar, so we've been using it as a selling point! Everyone loves Snappy Sister, Stormy Sister, and of course me, Spirited Sister!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does that work?

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: People love a nice story about a trio of sisters coming together! So yes, it works rather well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That seems... unethical.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: It's all storytelling! Songs, appearance, history. We're telling a story and people want to hear it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure if people wanting to hear it makes it entirely ethical.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Is it made better if our songs are good?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now that you mention it... no of course it isn't.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Well, you're just no fun, iknami.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Oh, hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem a little down. What's up?

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: I just want to get back to the College of Bards. It's like my spark has gone out being away from such a creative environment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure you'll be back there before long.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: You think?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It doesn't hurt to be hopeful, does it? Not to mention, hope can be a great inspiration!

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Hope can be a great inspiration... Perfect! I'll use that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll use it?

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: To write our next song!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... Well, I'm happy to have helped.
